clinical_workflow.intended_use: Clinician-controlled documentation scaffold for walking aid review.
clinical_workflow.not_for: Not for autonomous diagnosis, treatment selection, dosing, disposition, admission/discharge, procedural, imaging, referral, or emergency management decisions.

source_phase1.reason_for_inclusion: Common Geriatrics documentation scenario suitable as clinician-controlled note scaffolding rather than autonomous clinical advice.
source_phase1.distinct_purpose: Distinct documentation workflow for clinician-selected details around walking aid review.
source_phase1.duplicate_check_notes: Cleaned Phase 1 candidate. Exact ID is unique within this revised index and must be checked against existing_workflow_ids.json and alias inventory before import; no close baseline workflow assigned in this cleaned index.

safety_notes: No autonomous diagnosis or treatment recommendation is intended.
Red flags are documentation prompts only.
red_flag_prompts: severe or rapidly worsening symptoms documented if assessed
abnormal vital signs documented if measured
new neurological, cardiorespiratory, or systemic concern documented if assessed
clinician concern requiring escalation documented if present

chips.text: Walking aid review interval history documented
onset/duration documented if discussed
chips.evidence_relationship: The exact section supports documenting gait, balance, muscle strength, function, footwear, mobility, neurological, vision, cognition, medicines and support context during review of a walking aid without prescribing or fitting one.

history_draft.text: Walking aid review. [Interval history]. [Current symptoms/concerns]. [Change since last review]. [Relevant negatives]. [Clinician-documented discussion]. [Additional history].

exam_prompts.text: Documented if assessed or measured. Vital signs documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Functional/cognitive screen documentation documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Falls/frailty observations documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Medication/support context documented.

investigation_options.text: Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered Medication list or reconciliation documentation reviewed.
investigation_options.output_text: Medication list or reconciliation documentation reviewed.

investigation_options.text: Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Falls/cognition/function screen results documented.
Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered Caregiver or care-home records reviewed.

plan_options.text: Safety-netting documented if discussed by clinician.
Follow-up documented if arranged by clinician.
Patient questions or counseling discussion documented if discussed.
plan_options.output_text: Patient questions or counseling discussion documented.

clinical_blockers: Aid type, fit, condition, technique, environment, falls, examination, adjustment, replacement, training, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
